rent.note: furnished 1BR off-season or shoulder season; sea-view adds $100–$200; summer prices spike sharply

utilities.note: electric + water + home internet; A/C use drives summer bills

groceries.note: home cooking with local produce; imported goods costlier

diningOut.note: local tavernas and seafood; beachfront/tourist areas higher in summer

transit.note: furgons, local taxis, walking; no formal monthly transit product

coworking.note: limited dedicated coworking; budget assumes cafe working plus occasional paid workspace

simData.note: Albanian SIM; Vodafone or One; cheap plans

misc.note: gym, toiletries, beach activities, entertainment, buffer

accomSeasonality._doc: 12 monthly multipliers for RENT ONLY (Jan..Dec). 1.0 = base. Coastal gem peaks sharply Jun–Aug.

evidence.claim: Sarandë average cost is below many EU beach towns, but sea-view and summer premiums matter
evidence.quote: Sarandë's $891 average cost reflects its popularity as a coastal hotspot. On the Riviera (Sarandë) with a sea-view apartment add $100–$200 to the Tirana base budget of $900-$1,500/month

evidence.claim: Albania nomad budget €800–1,500/mo; higher comfort tier includes sea view and coworking assumptions
evidence.quote: Typical cost of living in Albania for a nomad: €800–1,500/month (comfort level €1,200–1,500 with sea view & coworking)

evidence.claim: Sarandë stays reasonable versus western Europe, but rents rise during tourist season
evidence.quote: While higher than Albania's average, it remains reasonable for its scenic location. Expect higher rent during tourist seasons

evidence.claim: budget travelers can live cheaply in Albania when cooking and avoiding peak tourist spend
evidence.quote: Living in Albania Under €40/Day: Digital Nomad Budget Guide
